code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to clause fragments (subject omitted)
    # Mapping of predicates to readable fragments.
    # If a predicate is not present here we fall back to a generic conversion.
    clause_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        # Additional military/administrative predicates
        "attackAircraft": "operates the attack aircraft {object}",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates the fighter aircraft {object}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates the helicopter {object}",
        "transportAircraft": "operates the transport aircraft {object}",
        "battle": "has participated in the battle {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {object}",
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "language": "speaks {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "headquarter": "has its headquarter at {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {object}",
        "hubAirport": "uses {object} as a hub airport",
        # fallback generic will be applied for any other predicate
    }

    def generic_clause(predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
        """Create a readable clause for predicates not in clause_map."""
        # Convert camelCase / PascalCase / snake_case to spaced words
        import re
        words = re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', ' ', predicate).replace('_', ' ')
        words = words.lower()
        return f"{words} {obj}"
    # Group triples by their subject to build relative clauses
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subj, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Main clause uses the first predicate
        first = group[0]
        tmpl = clause_map.get(first.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            main_clause = tmpl.format(object=first.object)
        else:
            main_clause = generic_clause(first.predicate, first.object)
        phrase = f"{subj} {main_clause}"
        # Additional predicates become relative clauses introduced by “which”
        for t in group[1:]:
            tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                rel = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            else:
                rel = generic_clause(t.predicate, t.object)
            phrase += f", which {rel}"
        # Capitalize the first character of each subject clause
        phrase = phrase[0].upper() + phrase[1:]
        sentences.append(phrase)

    if not sentences:
        return ""
    # Join all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    combined = ", and ".join(sentences)
    # Ensure the final sentence starts with a capital letter and ends with a period
    combined = combined[0].upper() + combined[1:]
    if not combined.endswith('.'):
        combined += '.'
    return combined

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
